response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack software developer with nearly two decades of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and product engineering rather than the hardware performance domain this role requires. He has no demonstrated experience in SoC/IP modeling, GPU or accelerator architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design. His low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby (DLang, async I/O, high-throughput services) is the closest touchpoint but is software-level performance, not silicon-level architecture. He meets the years-of-experience bar with a BS in Computer Science, yet the core technical domain mismatch makes him unsuitable for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: 18+ years of software engineering experience, exceeding the BS + 8 years requirement
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, open-source authorship, C/C++ exposure at National Instruments
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Low-latency systems and performance tuning experience
